approach: one spec at a time; the retired golden-set is reference only, nothing is migrated or copied
the CFG is the source of truth — update the spec, draw the control-flow graph, then re-derive the whole scenario set from the graph edge by edge; do not read the suite and patch only the gaps
a legacy case is a claim to verify against the current implementation, never evidence of current behavior
additive scenarios only on the frozen suite; any narrowing would be Clearance-bound and stop the mission
self-assert the spec gate within leash, bring the impl gate to the owner
blast: low — one node's README only; the frozen suite is unchanged, no scenario added; no engine, no shared instrument, no other suite touched; the implementation was not changed
reversibility: high — a docs-only README rebuild on an unmerged branch, revertable without touching any contract
novelty: low — the node, its suite and its implementation all already exist; this redraws the node spec to the four-section shape and confirms the suite against the graph
confidence: high — cold spec-judge re-derived every oracle and returned ALIGNED; the CFG re-drive confirmed the projection is bijective with no scenario change

why: Re-drove the 15-scenario suite from the manage SKILL.md control-flow graph — the intake fork (named operation takes the fast path with no menu / bare invocation gathers via an AskUserQuestion menu of at most four options), the four routes (manage-model-runners, list-skills, repair-private-skills, manage-skill-dirs), the three redirects for non-manage intents (authoring to define-*, scoring to the eval-run skills, change-spec to start-mission), the in-session load (spawns nothing, picks no model), and the three framing guards (opens no CR / invokes no gate, writes no status or approval, loads no governance / holds no production logic). Every edge binds one-to-one to a frozen scenario and the 15-scenario suite is a bijective projection of the graph — CFG-complete — so no scenario was added, modified, or removed. README-only change rebuilding the node spec to the four-section shape (What / Use Cases / Control Flow with a mermaid CFG / Scenario map). A fresh cold spec-judge re-derived every oracle independently and ran a mutant per fork (always-menu, route-authoring-to-an-engine, spawn-a-subagent, manage-picks-the-model, silently-truncate-to-four, handle-scoring-as-manage, handle-change-spec-as-manage, mis-route): all killed, verdict ALIGNED with no blocker. One cosmetic sync applied — the skill-dirs CFG edge label now carries the SKILL's full verb set (list / add / remove / preview). Root project status and its human ratification left untouched.

note: Filed #347. manage declares Fit:strong (classification is non-deterministic judgment, so the agent-behavior eval layers carry signal) yet its suite is 15 untagged boolean scenarios with no @rubric graded-behavior and no @trigger activation Scenario Outline. Defensible — routing is captured booleanly, each route scenario discriminates against a plausible mis-route, and the three redirects are genuine near-misses — so not a spec-gate failure. But either the near-miss activation judgment is under-exercised or Fit overstates the layer signal for a thin router. Resolve by adding @trigger near-miss activation scenarios or re-examining the Fit; frozen suite, out of scope for this additive/docs-only pass.
